category_title: Elementary EMLLs experiencing internalizing anxiety, withdrawal, and home-based distress
scenario_title: Whole-class reading comprehension with prolonged English listening
minimal_context: The student is participating in a 30-minute whole-class reading of the picture book 'What Do You Do with an Idea?' and is asked to discuss the story.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: The teacher has been reading 'What Do You Do with an Idea?' to the class for 30 minutes. Do you know what is happening in the story?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Makes a weak smile and nods quietly.)

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: That's good. What did the boy do with his idea?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Sits quietly and says nothing, with a depressed and helpless look on her face.)

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: You can tell me. What do you think?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: Umm…honestly…I have no idea what she’s talking about. I have been sitting quietly just like this. (Watches her classmates and gets up from her seat to move to the same area of the classroom when they do.)

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: The teacher has been reading 'What Do You Do with an Idea?' to the class for 30 minutes. Do you know what is happening in the story?

STUDENT: (Makes a weak smile and nods quietly.)

TUTOR: That's good. What did the boy do with his idea?

STUDENT: (Sits quietly and says nothing, with a depressed and helpless look on her face.)

TUTOR: You can tell me. What do you think?

STUDENT: Umm…honestly…I have no idea what she’s talking about. I have been sitting quietly just like this. (Watches her classmates and gets up from her seat to move to the same area of the classroom when they do.)
